title: The Link Between Sleep Deprivation and Brain Fog After 40
tags: sleep deprivation, brain fog, cognitive health, women over 40, sleep, memory, fatigue
summary: Chronic sleep deprivation after 40 impairs the brain's glymphatic waste clearance system, fragments memory consolidation, and elevates neuroinflammation, but targeted sleep hygiene and supplementation strategies can meaningfully restore cognitive clarity.
featured_image_alt: Woman walking in morning sunlight outdoors to support circadian rhythm and better sleep after 40
